deliverable: agency process overview / capabilities deck slide
content: Klimt & Design is a premium design agency that works with clients on branding, web, and product design projects. This slide outlines their internal operating model under the heading 'How we work', detailing four key principles: (1) 'One point of contact'—a Creative Director/Delivery Lead manages day-to-day client interactions; (2) 'Small fixed team'—a multidisciplinary team supports the Delivery Lead in delivering quality; (3) 'Communication'—they communicate asynchronously or synchronously with regular check-ins; (4) 'Figma'—designs are shared in Figma or familiar formats. An organizational chart on the right shows the hierarchy: Creative Director/Delivery Lead at top, with Visual Design Lead and Developer reporting below, and a Visual Designer nested under Visual Design Lead.

Each discrepancy includes linked document references and icon buttons for navigation.

visual_style_raw: Soft pastel color palette with coral-red, lavender-blue, and pale yellow icon backgrounds; clean sans-serif typography in black with light gray body text; minimalist icons and plenty of white space creating an approachable, methodical aesthetic.
deliverable: design review framework documentation
content: Klimt & Design presents its internal design feedback methodology and review categories used across client projects. The slide outlines three core feedback types: Architecture (app architecture and user flow recommendations), Usability (screen-level UI design evaluation based on usability heuristics, accessibility, and continuity), and Visual Design (decorative design issues that detract from professionalism and brand alignment). Each category is represented by a simple icon in pastel backgrounds—squares for Architecture, a figure for Usability, and a palette for Visual Design. The methodology references Jakob Nielsen's usability heuristics, Nielsen Norman Group UX research, Human Interface Guidelines, and Material Design 3 as foundational sources.

content: AEROLINK Tecnologías provides a unified operating system for aircraft fueling operations, replacing manual guesswork and silos in critical aviation logistics. The left slide shows the problem: 'Critical operations still run on guesswork and silos' with technical line drawings of aircraft fuel systems, storage tanks, and infrastructure, plus five red-badged pain points ('Disconnected teams', 'No real-time integration', 'Fragmented data records', 'Risky decisions by humans', 'Manual visibility'). The right slide presents the solution—'A unified operating system for aircraft fueling'—with a dark navy background, an aircraft illustration surrounded by green dot nodes and connectors representing system components, AEROLINK branding, and four capability bubbles (fuel quantity, management, monitoring, integration) plus five green-checkmark bullet points listing sustainability, compliance, and scaling benefits. Typography is modern sans-serif; color palette contrasts white and gray line art on the left against deep navy and bright green on the right for visual hierarchy.
